categories.name: Lower Division CS Core
categories.description: All five lower-division CS core courses are required.

categories.name: Calculus Sequence
categories.description: Choose one calculus sequence: MATH 19A+19B or MATH 20A+20B.

categories.name: Linear Algebra
categories.description: Choose one linear algebra course: AM 10 (Engr. Math Methods I) or MATH 21 (Linear Algebra).

categories.name: Upper Division Core (Algorithms)
categories.description: Choose CSE 101 (Intro to Data Structures & Algorithms) or CSE 101P (Python-based). CSE 101 requires CSE 13S; CSE 101P does not.

categories.name: Breadth Courses
categories.description: Pick 3 courses total from Breadth Lists A (not requiring CSE 101) and B (requiring CSE 101) combined.

categories.name: Disciplinary Communication
categories.description: Pick one DC course. DC courses cannot be used to satisfy any Upper Division Elective requirement.
categories.note: CSE 185S/CSE 185E is listed as a single option on the chart. CSE 195 (Senior Thesis) cannot count as both DC and capstone.

categories.name: Comprehensive Requirement (Capstone/Exit)
categories.description: Pass one Capstone Course or complete a Senior Thesis (CSE 195). The capstone course can also satisfy a Breadth or Upper Division Elective requirement.
categories.note: CSE 195 cannot count as both DC and capstone. Many capstone courses require additional prerequisites beyond major requirements.

categories.name: Upper Division Electives
categories.description: Three additional 5-credit (or more) upper division CSE elective courses (100-189), with at least 1 satisfying the Comprehensive Requirement. Students may substitute up to two CSE electives with approved non-CSE courses from the elective list. DC courses (CSE 115A, CSE 185E, CSE 185S) cannot count here. The chart specifies 3 elective slots, but 1 must be a capstone, leaving 2 additional electives.
categories.note: The full elective pool includes any 5-credit UD Baskin Engineering course (100-189, excluding 191-194, 196-199, CSE 115A, CSE 185E, CSE 185S) plus the listed non-engineering courses. Capstone courses CAN double-count as electives.
